macroDesigner.intro: Simulate G-code offline — paste or write a program and see the toolhead path, bounding box, travel and extrusion totals, and a time estimate, without touching the printer. Browse the built-in macro library for reference.

steps: Type or paste G-code in the editor (a sample square is loaded to start).
Press Simulate — the 2D path and the stats update.
Solid lines are extrusion moves; faint dashed lines are travel.
Browse the macro library and Insert one to start from its commands.

simulator.title: The simulator
simulator.body: It runs the program on a virtual toolhead: G0/G1 moves, G90/G91 (absolute/relative), M82/M83 (extruder mode), G92 (set position) and G28 (home → origin). It reports the path, the bounding box, total travel and extrusion, a rough time estimate from the feedrates, and a per-command timeline. Nothing is sent to the printer.

motion.title: Path & time
motion.body: The 2D view is the XY path (Z is in the bounds). The time estimate divides each move's length by its feedrate — it ignores acceleration, so it's a rough lower bound, not the exact print time. Commands the simulator doesn't model (e.g. M-codes, high-level macros) are listed as warnings and skipped.

macros.title: Macro library
macros.body: A reference set of common Klipper macros with their name, what they do, and the config sections they need. "Insert" drops a macro's high-level commands into the editor as a starting point — note those expand further on the printer (full Jinja/macro expansion is coming).

gcode.def: The line-by-line motion language the printer runs (moves, temperatures, fan, …).

feedrate.def: Move speed in mm/min — F1200 is 20 mm/s. Sets how fast each move runs.

macro.def: A named, reusable command (e.g. QUAD_GANTRY_LEVEL) that expands to a sequence of G-code.

path.title: Toolhead path (XY)
path.noPath: Run a simulation to see the path.

explain.title: Explain this macro
explain.travel: Rapid travel to X{x} Y{y} at {f} mm/min
explain.print: Print move — extrude {e} mm over {d} mm to X{x} Y{y} at {f} mm/min
explain.note: Loops and conditionals are expanded the way the printer would; a macro that needs live printer state falls back to a literal preview.

live.label: From the printer
live.placeholder: Pick one of your installed macros…
live.hint: {n} macros found on the printer — pick one to load its G-code and simulate it.
live.unreachable: Printer unreachable — connect to load your installed macros.

limits.title: Out of bounds / over speed
limits.oob: Line {line}: {axis}={value} is outside the build area ({min}…{max} mm).
limits.overSpeed: Line {line}: {value} mm/s exceeds the printer's {limit} mm/s limit (Klipper will clamp it).

gcode_state_unbalanced.msg: SAVE_GCODE_STATE without a matching RESTORE_GCODE_STATE — the saved positioning/extruder mode is never restored.
gcode_state_unbalanced.fix: Add a RESTORE_GCODE_STATE (or remove the SAVE).

ends_relative.msg: The macro ends in relative mode (G91/M83) — the next command will move relatively.
ends_relative.fix: Add a final G90 / M82, or wrap the moves in SAVE_GCODE_STATE … RESTORE_GCODE_STATE.

extrude_before_home.msg: An extruding move happens before G28 — risks a cold or un-homed extrude.
extrude_before_home.fix: Home (G28) and heat the hotend before extruding.

compare.title: Compare (A / B)
compare.hint: Simulate a second program (B) and diff it against the current result (A).
compare.placeholder: Paste or write the B program…

scaffold.title: Generate START / END_PRINT
scaffold.hint: Build a START_PRINT and END_PRINT tailored to this printer — its homing, the leveling it actually has, a mesh step, and prime/park positions inside the real build area.
scaffold.writeNote: Appends both macros to the chosen file behind the confirm gate — a backup is taken first and writes are refused while printing. Refused if a macro with the same name already exists.
scaffold.savedBackup: Saved to {file} (backup: {backup}). Restart firmware to apply.

note.bounds: Build area X {x0}-{x1}, Y {y0}-{y1} (prime/park fit inside).
note.leveling_none: No quad_gantry_level / z_tilt configured — left a placeholder.
note.no_bed: No heated bed — bed heating steps omitted.

flow.title: Flow check (hotend ceiling)
flow.hint: Pick your hotend from the catalog and every printing move is checked against its real melt-zone ceiling — an over-flowing macro shows up before it prints.
flow.ok: All printing moves are within the {ceiling} mm³/s ceiling.
